message:
feat(doctor): expand seed RPC integration test coverage (+45 lines)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

Rationale: extras are used by demos and feature-gated runtime; coverage here reduces regressions for visual effects and utility helpers. This epic adds real tests for each low-coverage module.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Each extras module in scope has deterministic unit tests covering core behavior + edge cases; no mocks/fakes.\n2) Unit tests include rich assertion context (inputs/expected/actual) and debugging logs where helpful.\n3) Visual FX tests validate determinism and rendering-critical invariants.\n4) E2E visual-fx sweep added with JSONL logs and fixed seeds/time.","notes":"2026-02-05 CoralBarn: All child tasks closed (visual_fx sampling/plasma/metaballs, filepicker/diagram/image/logging/live/timer/filesize coverage) plus E2E visual_fx sweep (bd-1qyn3). Epic criteria satisfied; ted_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-11fz2","depends_on_id":"bd-nq01e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-11hb","title":"Unit Tests: ftui-render diff/presenter/dirty-spans","description":"Scope: Add deterministic unit/integration tests for ftui-render diff strategy selection (Full/DirtyRows/FullRedraw), dirty-span stats/coverage, tile fallback behavior, and presenter state tracking. Use real Buffer/Frame instances and real diff computations (no mocks).

Considerations: Cover edge cases (0 width/height, span overflow, full-dirty rows), verify evidence/log outputs where applicable, and ensure selector stability under fixed workloads.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Tests cover all diff strategy branches and dirty-span stats paths.
2) Tile fallback and presenter state transitions validated with real buffers.
3) No mocks/fakes used; tests deterministic and pass clippy pedantic.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:13:36.996400038Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T23:48:54.655735529Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T23:48:54.655714109Z","close_reason":"Added dirty-span stats tests and presenter reset style reapply test.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11hb","depends_on_id":"bd-2dui","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T16:31:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-11hgk","title":"e2e/pty_run: ensure controlling TTY + faster drain/optional capture for high-output TUIs","description":"Problem
- `tests/e2e/lib/pty.sh` `pty_run` currently spawns the child via `subprocess.Popen(... start_new_session=True ...)` with inherited slave stdio. In some cases (notably `ftui-demo-showcase --vfx-harness`), the child fails with `os error 6 (ENXIO)` when opening `/dev/tty`, implying it has no controlling TTY.
- When capturing high-throughput ANSI output, the current reader loop (`read_poll` + 4k chunks) can backpressure the PTY and inflate `present_ms_*`, polluting perf measurements (root cause for bd-t153k).

Scope
- Fix `pty_run` so the child process reliably has a controlling TTY bound to the slave PTY (so `/dev/tty` works).
- Improve drain performance (drain-until-EAGAIN, larger reads) and add an opt-in mode to *discard* capture while still draining (to avoid perf backpressure).
- Default behavior must remain unchanged for existing e2e scripts unless the opt-in env var is set.

Acceptance Criteria
1) `pty_run` can run `ftui-demo-showcase --vfx-harness --vfx-perf` without `/dev/tty` ENXIO failures.
2) New opt-in env (e.g. `PTY_CAPTURE_MODE=none` or `PTY_CAPTURE_MAX_BYTES`) prevents perf backpressure while still draining output.
3) Existing e2e scripts using `pty_run` continue to pass.

Validation
- Run at least one existing e2e script that uses `pty_run` (or a representative `pty_run` invocation) and confirm exit 0.
- Add/extend a minimal deterministic check path proving `/dev/tty` is openable inside the child session.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"TealGrove","created_at":"2026-02-10T06:10:55.869269019Z","created_by":"TealGrove","updated_at":"2026-02-10T06:31:48.332914373Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T06:31:48.332894626Z","close_reason":"Completed (pty_run controlling TTY + drain-only capture + /dev/tty E2E check landed)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","performance","tests"],"comments":[{"id":16,"issue_id":"bd-11hgk","author":"FrostyCat","text":"Validated current `tests/e2e/lib/pty.sh` pty_run against `ftui-demo-showcase --vfx-harness --vfx-perf`.

Results:
- controlling TTY: exit 0, no /dev/tty ENXIO
- drain-only capture: `PTY_CAPTURE_MAX_BYTES=0` -> output_bytes=0, exit 0
- bounded capture: `PTY_CAPTURE_MAX_BYTES=200000` -> output_bytes=200000, exit 0

Artifacts:
- capture0 output: `/data/tmp/pty_bd-11hgk_vfx_capture0.pty` (0 bytes)
- capture0 JSONL: `/data/tmp/bd-11hgk_vfx_capture0.jsonl`
- cap200k output: `/data/tmp/pty_bd-11hgk_vfx_cap200k_1770704518.pty` (200000 bytes)
- cap200k JSONL: `/data/tmp/bd-11hgk_vfx_cap200k_1770704518.jsonl`","created_at":"2026-02-10T06:23:50Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-11ngr","title":"task(doctor-asupersync): migrate seed-demo networking, retries, and wait logic","description":"Background:
Seed/demo flows currently contain networking, retries, polling, and waits that are better expressed as structured tasks with explicit deadlines and evidence.

Goal:
Migrate seed/demo networking and retry/wait logic onto Asupersync-backed orchestration.","design":"Work packages:
- Identify network/retry/wait paths in doctor seed flows.
- Re-express them with explicit deadlines, cancellation, retry policy, and telemetry.
- Preserve CLI behavior and evidence outputs.
- Add fixtures for transient failures, permanent failures, and timeouts.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Networking and retry behavior become easier to reason about and test.
2. Unit/integration/E2E tests cover happy, retry, failure, timeout, and cancellation paths with detailed logs.
3. Artifact manifests retain or improve their current usefulness.","notes":"This should reduce “mystery waiting” and improve operator trust in seed failures.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-03-10T01:34:05.483431752Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-17T03:27:22.683309263Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync-frankentui","doctor_frankentui","network","retry"]}

Cover parameter ranges and determinism with fixed seeds/time.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Deterministic unit tests cover plasma effect output invariants and edge cases.\n2) Assertions/logs include seed, parameters, and expected vs actual hashes.\n3) No mocks/fakes; fixed seeds/time.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:15:37.054529497Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T01:04:54.553491382Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T01:04:54.553473248Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-120n1","title":"Demo Showcase: char-level match highlighting in Virtualized Search","description":"Implement character-level match highlighting in the Virtualized Search demo list.

Currently render_highlighted_row() uses whole-row styling when positions is non-empty. Add per-character highlighting for the matched indices (from fuzzy_match) by building ftui_text::Text spans and rendering via Paragraph.

Keep selection semantics: selected rows retain the selection background; matched characters should be visually distinct (gold + bold) in both selected and unselected states. Score suffix (e.g. " [123]") should not be highlighted.","acceptance_criteria":"Matched characters in the list row are rendered with MATCH_HIGHLIGHT (and bold), without changing cell content.
- Non-matched characters preserve existing base row styling (selected vs non-selected behavior unchanged).
- Add a unit test that renders a row and asserts fg colors at matched vs non-matched cell positions.
- Quality gates: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test -p ftui-demo-showcase all pass.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"BlueLake","created_at":"2026-02-10T19:07:20.216898832Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T19:17:33.130457099Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T19:17:33.130437983Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-122ks","title":"Add comprehensive tests for ftui-web/src/lib.rs","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:29:17.920061371Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:38.031288101Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:38.031268956Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":17,"issue_id":"bd-122ks","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added 27 new tests (36 total) covering: WebBackendError (display, std::error trait, eq), DeterministicClock (set/default/clone), WebEventSource (set_size, drain_events, features, empty read), WebPresenter (default, outputs/outputs_mut, take_outputs, capabilities, present_ui_owned), WebBackend (construction, Backend trait), patch_batch_stats (empty, cell counting), patch_batch_hash (empty determinism), build_patch_runs (full repaint, no diff), WebOutputs (default), cell_to_patch (empty, ASCII).","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:30:37Z"}]}
